name: Brainstorm Project
description: Expert Guided Facilitation through a single or multiple techniques

name: Market Research
description: Market analysis competitive landscape customer needs and trends

name: Domain Research
description: Industry domain deep dive subject matter expertise and terminology

name: Technical Research
description: Technical feasibility architecture options and implementation approaches

name: Create Brief
description: A guided experience to nail down your product idea

name: Create PRD
description: Expert led facilitation to produce your Product Requirements Document

name: Validate PRD
description: Validate PRD is comprehensive lean well organized and cohesive

name: Edit PRD
description: Improve and enhance an existing PRD

name: Create UX
description: Guidance through realizing the plan for your UX, strongly recommended if a UI is a primary piece of the proposed project

name: Create Architecture
description: Guided Workflow to document technical decisions

name: Create Epics and Stories
description: Create the Epics and Stories Listing

name: Check Implementation Readiness
description: Ensure PRD UX Architecture and Epics Stories are aligned

name: Sprint Planning
description: Generate sprint plan for development tasks - this kicks off the implementation phase by producing a plan the implementation agents will follow in sequence for every story in the plan.

name: Sprint Status
description: Anytime: Summarize sprint status and route to next workflow

name: Create Story
description: Story cycle start: Prepare first found story in the sprint plan that is next, or if the command is run with a specific epic and story designation with context. Once complete, then VS then DS then CR then back to DS if needed or next CS or ER

name: Validate Story
description: Validates story readiness and completeness before development work begins

name: Dev Story
description: Story cycle: Execute story implementation tasks and tests then CR then back to DS if fixes needed

name: QA Automation Test
agent-command: bmad:more direct approach than the advanced Test Architect module.:agent:qa
description: Generate automated API and E2E tests for implemented code using the project's existing test framework (detects existing well known in use test frameworks). Use after implementation to add test coverage. NOT for code review or story validation - use CR for that.

name: Code Review
description: Story cycle: If issues back to DS if approved then next CS or ER if epic complete

name: Retrospective
description: Optional at epic end: Review completed work lessons learned and next epic or if major issues consider CC

name: Document Project
description: Analyze an existing project to produce useful documentation

name: Generate Project Context
description: Scan existing codebase to generate a lean LLM-optimized project-context.md containing critical implementation rules patterns and conventions for AI agents. Essential for brownfield projects.

name: Quick Dev
description: Unified intent-in code-out workflow: clarify plan implement review and present

name: Correct Course
description: Anytime: Navigate significant changes. May recommend start over update PRD redo architecture sprint planning or correct epics and stories

name: Write Document
description: Describe in detail what you want, and the agent will follow the documentation best practices defined in agent memory. Multi-turn conversation with subprocess for research/review.

name: Update Standards
description: Update agent memory documentation-standards.md with your specific preferences if you discover missing document conventions.

name: Mermaid Generate
description: Create a Mermaid diagram based on user description. Will suggest diagram types if not specified.

name: Validate Document
description: Review the specified document against documentation standards and best practices. Returns specific actionable improvement suggestions organized by priority.

name: Explain Concept
description: Create clear technical explanations with examples and diagrams for complex concepts. Breaks down into digestible sections using task-oriented approach.

name: Brainstorming
description: Generate diverse ideas through interactive techniques. Use early in ideation phase or when stuck generating ideas.

name: Party Mode
description: Orchestrate multi-agent discussions. Use when you need multiple agent perspectives or want agents to collaborate.

name: bmad-help
description: Get unstuck by showing what workflow steps come next or answering BMad Method questions.

name: Index Docs
description: Create lightweight index for quick LLM scanning. Use when LLM needs to understand available docs without loading everything.

name: Shard Document
description: Split large documents into smaller files by sections. Use when doc becomes too large (>500 lines) to manage effectively.

name: Editorial Review - Prose
description: Review prose for clarity, tone, and communication issues. Use after drafting to polish written content.
outputs: three-column markdown table with suggested fixes

name: Editorial Review - Structure
description: Propose cuts, reorganization, and simplification while preserving comprehension. Use when doc produced from multiple subprocesses or needs structural improvement.

name: Adversarial Review (General)
description: Review content critically to find issues and weaknesses. Use for quality assurance or before finalizing deliverables. Code Review in other modules run this automatically, but its useful also for document reviews

name: Edge Case Hunter Review
description: Walk every branching path and boundary condition in code, report only unhandled edge cases. Use alongside adversarial review for orthogonal coverage - method-driven not attitude-driven.

name: Distillator
description: Lossless LLM-optimized compression of source documents. Use when you need token-efficient distillates that preserve all information for downstream LLM consumption.
output-location: adjacent to source document or specified output_path

name: Brainstorming
sequence: Facilitate brainstorming sessions using one or more techniques.

name: Design Thinking
sequence: Guide human-centered design processes using empathy-driven methodologies.

name: Innovation Strategy
sequence: Identify disruption opportunities and architect business model innovation.

name: Problem Solving
sequence: Apply systematic problem-solving methodologies to crack complex challenges.

name: Storytelling
sequence: Craft compelling narratives using proven story frameworks and techniques.

name: Quick Prototype
sequence: Rapid game prototyping to test mechanics and ideas quickly.

name: Brainstorm Game
sequence: Facilitate game brainstorming sessions with game-specific context and techniques.

name: Check Implementation Readiness
sequence: Ensure GDD UX Architecture and Epics Stories are aligned before production begins.

name: Code Review
sequence: Perform thorough clean context QA code review on stories flagged Ready for Review.

name: Correct Course
sequence: Navigate significant changes during game dev sprint when implementation is off-track.

name: Create Epics and Stories
sequence: Create the Epics and Stories listing from GDD requirements. These are the specs that drive development.

name: Game Brief
sequence: Interactive game brief creation that guides users through defining their game vision.

name: Game Design Document
sequence: Create a GDD with mechanics systems progression and implementation guidance.

name: Narrative Design
sequence: Create comprehensive narrative documentation including story structure character arcs and world-building. Use for story-driven games.

name: Create PRD
sequence: Create a PRD from GDD or from scratch for use with external tools like bmad-assist.

name: Create Story
sequence: Create Story with comprehensive context for developer agent implementation.

name: Create UX Design
sequence: Guidance through realizing the plan for your game UX/UI, strongly recommended if UI is a primary piece of the proposed game.

name: Dev Story
sequence: Execute Dev Story workflow implementing tasks and tests.

name: Document Project
sequence: Analyze an existing game project to produce useful documentation.

name: Domain Research
sequence: Game industry domain deep dive subject matter expertise and terminology.

name: E2E Scaffold
sequence: Scaffold E2E testing infrastructure.

name: Edit PRD
sequence: Improve and enhance an existing PRD.

name: Game Architecture
sequence: Produce a scale-adaptive game architecture with engine systems networking and technical design.

name: Project Context
sequence: Create optimized project-context.md for chosen agentic tool consistency.

name: Market Research
sequence: Game market analysis competitive landscape player needs and trends.

name: Performance Test
sequence: Design performance testing strategy.

name: Playtest Plan
sequence: Create structured playtesting plan.

name: Quick Dev
sequence: Flexible game development with game-specific considerations.

name: Quick Dev New Preview
sequence: Unified quick flow (experimental): clarify intent plan implement review and present in a single workflow.

name: Quick Spec
sequence: Quick one-off tasks small changes simple apps utilities without extensive planning.

name: Retrospective
sequence: Facilitate team retrospective after a game development epic is completed.

name: Sprint Planning
sequence: Generate or update sprint-status.yaml from epic files.

name: Sprint Status
sequence: View sprint progress surface risks and get next action recommendation.

name: Technical Research
sequence: Technical feasibility game engine options and implementation approaches.

name: Test Automate
sequence: Generate automated game tests.

name: Test Design
sequence: Create comprehensive game test scenarios covering gameplay progression and quality requirements.

name: Test Framework
sequence: Initialize game test framework architecture for Unity Unreal Engine or Godot projects.

name: Test Review
sequence: Review test quality and coverage.

name: Validate PRD
sequence: Validate PRD against standards for external tool compatibility.

name: Teach Me Testing
sequence: Teach testing fundamentals through 7 sessions (TEA Academy).

name: ATDD
sequence: Generate failing tests (TDD red phase).

name: Test Automation
sequence: Expand test coverage.

name: CI Setup
sequence: Configure CI/CD quality pipeline.

name: Test Framework
sequence: Initialize production-ready test framework.

name: NFR Assessment
sequence: Non-functional requirements assessment.

name: Test Design
sequence: Risk-based test planning.

name: Test Review
sequence: Quality audit (0-100 scoring).

name: Traceability
sequence: Coverage traceability and gate.

name: Agentic Development
sequence: Iterative build-verify cycle with design log tracking and browser verification.

name: Alignment & Signoff
sequence: Stakeholder buy-in pitch and service agreement. Skip if building your own product.

name: Conceptual Sketching
sequence: Fast rough visual exploration before detailed specification. Skip for straightforward scenarios.

name: Conceptual Specifications
sequence: Document every design decision for every element on every page. Developers build directly from this.

name: Design Delivery
sequence: Validate specs are complete and package as DD yaml files for development handoff.

name: Design System
sequence: Manage component library and design tokens. Skip if Design System Mode None.

name: Wake Freya
sequence: UX Designer agent for Phases 3-4. Checks prerequisites and offers next steps.

name: Functional Components
sequence: Extract reusable patterns from specs into component definitions. Skip if Design System Mode None.

name: Outline Scenarios
sequence: Define user journeys as persona+goal+outcome. Each connects to a Trigger Map driving force.

name: Platform Requirements
sequence: Technical boundaries: platforms devices integrations constraints. Skip for simple landing pages.

name: Product Evolution
sequence: Continuous improvement: feedback to Trigger Map to spec update to code to verify.

name: Project Brief
sequence: Define product vision positioning and success criteria. Every design decision traces back to this.

name: Wake Saga
sequence: Strategic Analyst agent for Phases 1-2. Scans repos and offers next steps.

name: Storyboarding
sequence: Sequence the user journey through screens with entry points transitions and error paths. Skip for simple scenarios.

name: Trigger Mapping
sequence: Map business goals to user psychology. Create personas and score features against driving forces.

name: Acceptance Testing
sequence: Test on real users in their environment with retrospective think-aloud sessions.

name: Visual Design
sequence: Transform specs into styled HTML prototypes with Figma round-trips.
